command_palette.title: Command Palette
command_palette.confirm_reset_settings: Are you sure you want to reset all settings to defaults?
command_palette.quick_connect_resolve_failed: Failed to resolve SSH config alias '{{alias}}'

page.title: Connection Matrix
page.description: Visual topology of active SSH tunnels and jump hosts.
page.connect_hint: Connect to servers to populate the matrix.

controls.instructions: Scroll to zoom · Drag to pan · Double-click node for menu

plugin.manager_description: Native plugins use the native runtime (manifest-only / WASM / process); Tauri legacy-js plugins can be discovered, inspected, or uninstalled, but cannot run in the native build.

guide.webdav_description: For Jianguoyun and most WebDAV services, keep Endpoint at the DAV root and put your sync folder name in Namespace.
guide.http_json_description: Point Endpoint at your service root and choose any Namespace that should hold one encrypted snapshot stream.
guide.step_choose_backend: Choose the backend type that matches your server.
guide.step_fill_fields: Fill the connection fields and the sync password used to encrypt exported .oxide snapshots.
guide.step_save: Click Save Settings first so secrets are stored securely in the host keychain.
guide.step_check: Click Check Remote. On first use, 'No remote snapshot is available yet' is expected.
guide.step_upload: Click Upload Now to create the encrypted snapshot on the remote side.
guide.step_pull: On another device, use the same backend config and sync password, then click Pull and Preview before importing.
guide.webdav_duplicate_warning: Do not put the same folder path in both Endpoint and Namespace. If Endpoint already ends with your sync folder, keep Namespace unique instead of duplicating the same path.

conflict.blocked_description: Remote changes are newer than your last synced revision while local changes are still pending. Re-check the remote, pull first, or force an overwrite.

notes.upload_only: Auto sync only uploads. Remote updates always require user confirmation.
notes.untrusted_remote: Remote storage should be treated as untrusted. The server only stores encrypted .oxide blobs.
notes.secrets_not_synced: Passwords, passphrases, and plugin secrets are never uploaded as server-readable plaintext.
notes.secrets_leave_blank: Secret fields stay blank on load. Leave them blank to keep stored values unchanged, or enter a new value to replace the stored secret.
notes.backend_dropbox: Dropbox uses the namespace field as a folder path and requires an app access token.
notes.backend_git: Git uses a GitHub Contents API compatible endpoint. Set Endpoint to your API root, Repository to owner/repo, Branch to the target branch, and Namespace as an optional folder path inside the repository.
notes.backend_s3: S3 Compatible uses the endpoint as the service root, bucket as the container, and namespace as an optional object prefix.

errors.operation_in_progress: Another cloud sync operation is already running.
errors.missing_endpoint: Configure the backend endpoint before syncing.
errors.missing_namespace: Configure the namespace or path prefix before syncing.
errors.missing_backend_token: Configure the backend access token before syncing.
errors.http_unauthorized: The remote service returned 401 Unauthorized. Check the WebDAV Basic username/password or access token.
errors.network_request_failed: Network request failed. This usually means the remote service rejected the request, the network is unavailable, or the current OxideTerm version is still falling back to browser fetch and hit a CORS restriction.
errors.missing_git_repository: Configure the Git repository before syncing.
errors.missing_s3_bucket: Configure the S3 bucket before syncing.
errors.missing_s3_region: Configure the S3 region before syncing.
errors.missing_s3_access_key_id: Configure the S3 access key ID before syncing.
errors.missing_s3_secret_access_key: Configure the S3 secret access key before syncing.
errors.missing_sync_password: Configure a sync password before uploading or importing snapshots.
errors.remote_changed_before_upload: Remote snapshot changed since the last known revision. Pull and preview before uploading again.
errors.remote_update_conflict_hint: Remote revision {{revision}} differs from your last known revision while local changes are still pending.
errors.etag_conflict_detected: The remote snapshot changed during upload. Check remote changes before retrying.
errors.snapshot_too_large: Remote snapshot exceeds the safety limit of {{limit}}.
errors.remote_not_found: No remote snapshot is available yet.
errors.rollback_backup_too_large: Local rollback backup is too large to store safely ({{size}}, limit {{limit}}).
errors.rollback_backup_missing: No local rollback backup is available.
errors.secret_unlock_required: Stored sync secrets need to be unlocked once with a manual action before auto upload can continue.
errors.secret_access_cancelled: Keychain access was canceled. Retry the action and approve Touch ID or system authentication.
errors.secret_access_failed: Failed to read the stored secret from secure storage.
errors.preflight_failed: Export preflight failed. Check whether all required key material is available.

toast.rollback_backup_available: A local rollback backup is available if you want to restore the previous state.
toast.restore_success_description: Imported {{imported}} items, merged {{merged}} items from the rollback backup.
toast.settings_saved_failed_title: Failed to save cloud sync settings

preview.embedded_keys: Embedded keys will be extracted during import.
preview.no_embedded_keys: No embedded keys were found in the remote snapshot.
preview.app_settings: Global OxideTerm settings are included in this snapshot.
preview.local_changes_warning: Local unsynced changes are still present. The plugin will create a rollback backup before import.
preview.record_rename: {{name}}: rename to {{target}} because the local name already exists
preview.record_skip: {{name}}: skip because a local connection with the same name already exists
preview.record_merge: {{name}}: merge into the existing local connection {{target}}

confirm.clear_secret_description: This removes the stored value for {{label}} from secure storage. This action cannot be undone.
confirm.restore_backup_description: This imports the rollback backup created at {{createdAt}} using replace mode.

placeholders.secret_stored: Stored securely. Enter a new value to replace it.
placeholders.git_access_token: GitHub PAT or compatible contents token

cloud_sync.native_service_pending: The native service is being wired to the built-in compatibility layer.
cloud_sync.native_service_ready: The built-in native sync service is connected to the compatibility layer.

plugin.native_description: Native plugin hosting is not enabled yet.
plugin.native_empty_title: Plugin system not available in native
plugin.native_empty_description: OxideTerm native keeps the plugin entry reserved while the non-WebView plugin API is designed. No plugin install, enable, marketplace, or runtime actions are exposed here yet.
plugin.native_webview_note: WebView: not used in the native plugin path
plugin.native_api_note: API: extension contract still under design
plugin.native_sidebar_empty_description: Open the plugin tab for current native status.

onboarding.subtitle: Rust-native SSH terminal client
onboarding.project_intro: OxideTerm is a modern, cross-platform SSH terminal client built entirely in Rust. Manage remote servers with multiplexed connections, integrated SFTP, smart port forwarding, AI-powered assistance, and a CLI companion tool — secure, fast, and extensible.
onboarding.highlight_performance_desc: Async I/O, WebGL rendering, zero-copy binary protocol.
onboarding.highlight_security_arch_desc: OS keychain, ChaCha20 encryption, pure Rust SSH stack.
onboarding.highlight_crossplatform_desc: macOS, Windows, and Linux with a native look and feel.
onboarding.highlight_extensible_desc: Plugin system, custom themes, MCP integration, CLI tool.
onboarding.theme_hint: Pick a terminal color scheme. You can always change this later in Settings.
onboarding.appearance_desc: Customize your terminal's look and feel. Theme and font can be changed later in Settings.
onboarding.font_hint: Fonts marked ✓ are bundled and ready to use.
onboarding.workflow_desc: One SSH connection powers your entire workflow.
onboarding.workflow_connect_desc: Add an SSH server with password, key, or certificate authentication.
onboarding.workflow_terminal_desc: Open multiple terminals with tabs and split panes.
onboarding.workflow_sftp_desc: Browse, upload, and download files via the built-in SFTP browser.
onboarding.workflow_forwarding_desc: Set up local, remote, or dynamic (SOCKS5) port forwards.
onboarding.workflow_ide_desc: Edit remote files with syntax highlighting and code completion.
onboarding.quick_start_desc: Get started right away — open a local terminal, connect to a server, or import your existing SSH config.
onboarding.shortcuts_hint: Master these shortcuts to boost your productivity.
onboarding.features_desc: Explore these powerful features to get the most out of OxideTerm.
onboarding.cmd_palette_desc: Access connections, settings, and themes — one shortcut away.
onboarding.ai_chat_desc: Sidebar chat, inline terminal assistant, and autonomous agent mode — ask questions, generate commands, analyze output.
onboarding.sftp_desc: Browse remote files, drag-and-drop upload/download with transfer queue.
onboarding.port_forwarding_desc: Local, remote, and SOCKS5 dynamic tunnels through SSH.
onboarding.reconnect_desc: 30s grace period — TUI apps like vim stay intact.
onboarding.cli_companion_desc: Use oxideterm to manage sessions, transfer files, and query AI from any terminal.
onboarding.multiplexing_desc: Terminal, SFTP, port forwarding share one SSH connection.
onboarding.security_desc: Keychain storage, ChaCha20-Poly1305 encryption, pure Rust SSH.
onboarding.local_file_manager_desc: Desktop file browser with Quick Look previews, bookmarks, and drag-drop.
onboarding.launchpad_desc: Native app launcher — search and open apps from /Applications instantly.
onboarding.wsl_graphics_desc: Run Linux GUI apps on Windows via built-in VNC — desktop or single-app mode.
onboarding.plugin_system_desc: Extend with custom tabs, terminal hooks, commands, and settings via ESM plugins.
onboarding.custom_themes_desc: 30+ built-in terminal themes, or create your own with JSON definitions.
onboarding.tip_settings: All settings are customizable — press {{shortcut}} to open Settings anytime.
onboarding.tip_multiplexing: Terminal, SFTP, and port forwarding share a single SSH connection via multiplexing — no extra authentication needed.
onboarding.tip_shortcuts: Press {{shortcut}} anytime to view the full keyboard shortcuts reference.
onboarding.cli_step_desc: Install the oxideterm command-line tool to control OxideTerm from any terminal.
onboarding.cli_step_what_text: A standalone CLI binary that communicates with the running OxideTerm app via IPC. Manage sessions, transfer files, query AI — all from your shell.
onboarding.cli_step_cap_sessions: List and manage SSH sessions & local terminals
onboarding.cli_step_cap_forward: Create and delete port forwarding rules
onboarding.cli_step_cap_ai: Ask AI questions with streaming Markdown output
onboarding.cli_step_cap_status: Query app status and health information
onboarding.cli_step_reinstall_hint: The installed CLI differs from the bundled CLI for OxideTerm {{version}}. Reinstall the bundled CLI to avoid protocol mismatches.
onboarding.cli_step_not_bundled: CLI binary not bundled in this build.
onboarding.cli_step_install_error: Installation failed. You can retry or install later from Settings.
onboarding.cli_step_skip_hint: You can always install it later from Settings → General → CLI Companion.
onboarding.disclaimer_desc: Please review the following terms before getting started.
onboarding.disclaimer_no_warranty_text: OxideTerm is provided "as is", without warranty of any kind. The entire risk as to the quality and performance of the software is with you.
onboarding.disclaimer_data_security_text: You are solely responsible for safeguarding your SSH credentials, private keys, and server configurations. The authors are not liable for data loss, security incidents, or unauthorized access.
onboarding.disclaimer_ai_text: AI-generated outputs are for informational purposes only and do not constitute professional advice. Always review AI-generated commands before execution. Terminal content may be sent to third-party AI providers when enabled.
onboarding.disclaimer_accept: I have read and agree to the above terms
onboarding.disclaimer_gpl_note: Full license terms: GPL-3.0 §15–16. See DISCLAIMER.md for details.
onboarding.ai_tools_desc: Enable AI-powered features to enhance your terminal workflow.
onboarding.ai_setup_desc: Choose which AI features to enable. You can always change these later in Settings.
onboarding.ai_tools_oxidesens_desc: A built-in AI assistant that understands your terminal context — ask questions, generate commands, analyze output, and automate tasks directly from the sidebar or inline in the terminal.
onboarding.ai_tools_cap_sidebar: Sidebar chat with terminal context awareness
onboarding.ai_tools_cap_inline: Inline terminal assistant for quick commands
onboarding.ai_tools_cap_agent: Autonomous agent mode for complex tasks
onboarding.ai_tools_cap_byok: Bring your own API key — works with any provider
onboarding.ai_tools_privacy_local: All processing happens locally — no data is collected by OxideTerm
onboarding.ai_tools_privacy_keys: API keys are stored securely in your OS keychain
onboarding.ai_tools_privacy_context: Terminal content is sent to your chosen AI provider only when you ask
onboarding.ai_tools_enable_hint: You can change this anytime in Settings → AI.
onboarding.ai_tools_enable_tools_hint: Allow the model to use built-in tools such as file reading, terminal inspection, and approved actions.
onboarding.ai_tools_enable_tools_warning: Tool use is managed separately from chat and remains permission-based. You can review per-tool approvals later in Settings → AI.
onboarding.ai_tools_cmd_palette_desc: Your universal shortcut — quickly access connections, settings, themes, and AI features. Press once to get started.
onboarding.ai_tools_later_hint: All AI and tool settings can be configured later in Settings. No API keys are required during setup.

zen_mode.hint_other: Zen Mode — Press Ctrl+Shift+Z to exit
